datasets.title: Estimates of Automatic Stabilizers
datasets.description: Deficit or surplus with and without CBO's estimate of automatic stabilizers, and related estimates of GDP and unemployment gaps.

notes.units: Sheet 1 values in billions_of_dollars; Sheet 2 values as percentage of potential GDP. GDP gap in billions. Unemployment gap in percentage points.
notes.automatic_stabilizers: Changes in federal revenues and outlays that occur automatically in response to cyclical movements in GDP and unemployment.
notes.gdp_gap: Difference between actual/projected GDP and CBO's estimate of potential GDP.
notes.unemployment_gap: Actual/projected unemployment rate minus CBO's estimate of the noncyclical rate of unemployment.
notes.quarterly_data: The source workbook also contains quarterly NIPA-based estimates (Sheets 4-5) which are not extracted here.

datasets.title: Revenue Projections, by Category
datasets.description: Projections of revenues by category, estimates of the effects of extending tax provisions scheduled to expire within ten years, and estimates of selected policy options affecting revenues.

notes.units: billions_of_dollars unless noted (GDP-share variables are percentages of GDP).
notes.fiscal_year: Federal fiscal year (Oct 1 through Sep 30). Sheets 1, 4, 5 use fiscal-year data.
notes.calendar_year: Sheets 3, 6, 8 use calendar-year data (CY prefix in date column).
notes.actual_year: The first column is the most recent actual year; remaining columns are projections.
notes.customs_note: In 2026-02, Customs duties is a standalone top-level category. In 2025-01 and earlier, it appears under the Other subgroup. The variable name rev_customs is used in both cases.
notes.field_footnotes: Some source tables use superscript letter footnotes on row labels. For supported tables, extracted note text is copied into each affected field under variable_note.

rev_iit_capital_gain_loss.variable_note: Includes sales of capital assets and sales of property other than capital assets.

rev_iit_business_income.variable_note: Business income is before disallowed losses on Form 461 and the deduction for qualifying business income.

rev_iit_pensions.description: Taxable pensions, annuities, and IRA distributions

rev_iit_other_income.variable_note: All sources of income or loss that are included in AGI but not reported above. Includes net operating losses from prior years, unemployment compensation, alimony received, taxable state and local tax refunds, gambling earnings, the exclusion for foreign-earned income, disallowed business losses, and many other smaller items.

rev_iit_agi.variable_note: Income groups are defined on the basis of the distribution of tax units by adjusted gross income.

rev_iit_itemized_deductions.description: Total itemized deductions (itemizers only, after limits)
rev_iit_itemized_deductions.variable_note: Amounts shown are after any applicable limits on each deduction, such as the limitation of the state and local tax (SALT) deduction, and after applying the overall limitation on itemized deductions.

rev_iit_total_exemptions_deductions.description: Total exemptions and deductions after limits
rev_iit_total_exemptions_deductions.variable_note: Total exemptions and deductions includes the deduction for qualifying business income and additional deductions. Limits include the overall limitation on itemized deductions.

rev_iit_taxable_income.variable_note: Taxable income is larger than AGI minus total deductions and exemptions because taxable income cannot fall below zero, even if deductions and exemptions exceed AGI.

rev_iit_taxable_ordinary.description: Taxable income taxed at ordinary rates (before AMT)
rev_iit_taxable_ordinary.variable_note: See the supplemental data on parameters for bracket thresholds.

rev_iit_taxable_capgains_divs.description: Taxable income taxed at reduced rates for capital gains and dividends

rev_iit_tax_before_credits.description: Total income tax (including AMT) before credits

rev_iit_tax_ordinary.description: Tax from taxable income taxed at ordinary rates
rev_iit_tax_ordinary.variable_note: See the supplemental data on parameters for the rate structure.

rev_iit_tax_capgains_divs.description: Tax from taxable income taxed at reduced rates for capital gains and dividends

rev_iit_tax_amt.description: Tax from AMT (including credits limited under the regular tax)

rev_iit_tax_after_credits.variable_note: Income tax after credits does not include the portion of certain refundable credits that are considered outlays.

rev_iit_returns_total.variable_note: Based on returns filed in 2022, including dependents, plus estimates of future additional filers. The estimate does not account for changes in the filing thresholds, which may cause changes in the number of filers. m. Includes taxpayers with liability under the AMT and some taxpayers for whom the AMT limits their credits taken under the regular income tax. n. Receipts are actuals through FY 2025 and projections thereafter. The final projections of individual income tax receipts include many adjustments to the tax year liability estimate. Those adjustments include converting the projections from a calendar year liability basis to a fiscal year receipts basis, adding receipts of back taxes and fiduciary taxes, accounting for reallocations made between individual income and payroll taxes, adjusting for certain federal subsidies that boost business income in the NIPAs (national income and product accounts) but not taxable income, adding the effects of certain legislation not explicitly modeled on the microsimulation tax model, and adjusting for differences in projected tax liabilities and those implied by recently observed collections.

rev_iit_returns_itemized.description: Number of returns with itemized deductions (millions)

rev_iit_returns_amt.description: Number of returns affected by AMT (millions)

rev_capgains_pct_iit.description: Capital gains tax receipts as a percentage of individual income tax receipts

rev_corp_profits_before_tax.description: Corporate profits before tax (without IVA and CCAdj)
rev_corp_profits_before_tax.variable_note: Gross measure related to current activity; BEA derives it from total receipts less total deductions, as reported by the IRS for all corporations, by adding and subtracting certain items treated differently in the national accounts and for income tax purposes. Component of BEA's measure of overall corporate profits (with IVA and CCAdj). Changes to tax depreciation affect estimates of profits before tax but not corporate profits with IVA and CCAdj. For more information on how changes to tax depreciation affect profits in the NIPAs, see Bureau of Economic Analysis, "How Do Changes in the Tax Treatment of Depreciation Impact NIPA Corporate Profits?" (August 5, 2011), www.bea.gov/help/faq/1002.

rev_corp_adj_misreporting.description: Adjustment for misreporting on income tax returns

rev_corp_adj_amendments.variable_note: Contains adjustments to reflect changes from Public Law 115-97, including the treatment of certain deductions such as for excess interest expense (beginning in 2018). For 2021, this item also contains an adjustment to reflect the effect of Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) loans in corporate profits. For more information see "How does the Paycheck Protection Program Impact the National Income and Product Accounts?", https://www.bea.gov/help/faq/1408. Previously this item contained and adjustment for changes to the treatment of research and experimentation expenses (beginning in 2022). That effect has been removed as of the current projection and is captured in the “Adjustment to depreciate expenditures for intellectual property products” included in "Other adjustments to corporate profits before tax".

rev_corp_adj_foreign_equity_net.description: Income from equities in foreign corporations and branches by all U.S. residents, net

rev_corp_adj_other.description: Other adjustments to corporate profits before tax
rev_corp_adj_other.variable_note: Includes the following elements of Table 7.16 in the NIPAs: “income of organizations not filing corporation income tax returns,” “depletion on domestic minerals,” “adjustment to depreciate expenditures for mining exploration, shafts and wells,” “adjustment to depreciate expenditures for intellectual property products,” “disaster adjustments (net),” “costs of trading or issuing corporate securities,” and “excess of employer expenses over actual employer contributions for defined benefit employee pension plans.” "Other adjustments to corporate profits before tax" also includes adjustments for any remaining discrepancies to align corporate profits before tax with total receipts less total deductions.

rev_corp_profits_after_adj.description: Corporate profits before tax after total adjustments (is equivalent to total receipts less total deductions, IRS)
rev_corp_profits_after_adj.variable_note: Estimate for C corporations as well as entities that are legally organized as corporations but whose income is not subject to the corporate income tax.

rev_corp_net_income_all.description: Net income (less deficit) for all corporations
rev_corp_net_income_all.variable_note: Includes the income of all entities legally organized as corporations, as well as additional income not captured in the derivation from profits, such as nontaxable interest on state and local government obligations and constructive taxable income from related foreign corporations.

rev_corp_net_income_subject.description: Net income (less deficit) for corporations subject to corporate income tax
rev_corp_net_income_subject.variable_note: Excludes the estimated net income of pass-through entities.

rev_corp_income_subject_to_tax.description: Income subject to tax under the corporate income tax
rev_corp_income_subject_to_tax.variable_note: Removes estimated net operating losses and other special deductions from the measure of "net income (less deficit) for corporations subject to the corporate income tax." In addition, it is adjusted to account for any discrepancies between derived and actual values in history.

datasets.title: Tax Parameters and Effective Marginal Tax Rates
datasets.description: Parameters used in CBO's microsimulation tax model and effective marginal tax rates on labor and capital.

notes.units: Varies by variable: dollar amounts, percentages, percentage points, index values, or counts.
notes.calendar_year: All values are on a calendar-year basis.
notes.filing_status_codes: single = Single; mfj = Married Filing Jointly; hoh = Head of Household; mfs = Married Filing Separately.
notes.eitc_child_codes: 0 = no qualifying children; 1 = one child; 2 = two children; 3plus = three or more children.
notes.vintage_variation: Some variables appear only in certain vintages (e.g., personal exemption and PEP thresholds in older vintages; delta EMTR variables in 2026-02 only).
notes.actual_year: The first column is the most recent actual year; remaining columns are projections.
